Organism: Chlorobaculum tepidum (strain ATCC 49652 / DSM 12025 / NBRC 103806 / TLS) (Chlorobium tepidum)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the radical SAM superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=8,12-diethyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 12-ethyl-8-propyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:49172, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:90964, ChEBI:CHEBI:90966; EC=2.1.1.332; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17586634}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=12-ethyl-8-propyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 12-ethyl-8-isobutyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:49176, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:90966, ChEBI:CHEBI:90967; EC=2.1.1.332; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17586634};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of the major light-harvesting pigment bacteriochlorophyll c (BChlc), which confers a significant competitive advantage to green sulfur bacteria living at limiting red and near-infrared light intensities. BchQ is a methyltransferase that adds two consecutive methyl groups to the ethyl carbon at the C-8(2) position of 8,12-diethyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d to yield 12-ethyl-8-isobutyl-3-vinylbacteriochlorophyllide d. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17586634}.
